summary: <cite index="3-14,7-1,7-11">The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29 in Chair Powell's final meeting before Kevin Warsh's anticipated confirmation, but the decision exposed rare internal fractures with four dissents—the most since 1992. Powell announced he will remain as a Fed governor beyond his May 15 chairmanship end date, citing ongoing legal pressures.</cite> <cite index="11-12,11-17,12-4">Equities surged regardless, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting all-time highs on May 1 as Apple beat earnings on strong iPhone 17 demand and Iran peace hopes drove oil lower.</cite> <cite index="36-8,36-10,36-22">Mega-cap tech earnings delivered a split verdict: Alphabet rallied 10% on cloud strength and raised capex to $190 billion, while Meta plunged 9% after hiking AI spending guidance to $125-145 billion, fueling investor anxiety over returns on the industry's $650 billion collective AI buildout.</cite> <cite index="22-1,29-5,53-9">Bitcoin held above $78,000 for the first time since February, supported by a reversal in spot ETF flows after three days of outflows, though crypto remains sensitive to macro crosscurrents.</cite>

signals.title: Fed signals hawkish hold with historic four-dissenter split
signals.desc: <cite index="3-8,5-28,10-14">Four FOMC members dissented—one favoring a cut, three opposing the easing bias in the statement—signaling deep division as elevated inflation tied to Middle East energy prices keeps rate cuts on hold through at least mid-2026.</cite>

signals.title: Apple crushes earnings on iPhone 17 'extraordinary' demand
signals.desc: <cite index="11-17,12-4,12-30">Apple beat Q2 estimates with gross margin of 49.3% (vs. 48.4% consensus), announced a new buyback, and provided upbeat guidance despite iPhone sales slightly missing, driving shares up 3%+ and pushing Nasdaq to records.</cite>

signals.title: Meta craters 9% on $145B AI capex blowout, user growth miss
signals.desc: <cite index="14-25,36-12,36-22">Meta raised full-year 2026 capex guidance to $125-145 billion while user growth disappointed, triggering concerns that AI spending is outpacing revenue generation and eroding investor confidence in return on investment.</cite>

signals.title: Bitcoin reclaims $78K on ETF inflow reversal, exchange reserve lows
signals.desc: <cite index="29-7,29-8,26-22,26-24">Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $4.5M net inflows after three days of losses, while April saw strongest monthly inflows since October 2025 and exchange reserves hit multi-year lows, signaling long-term holder conviction despite macro headwinds.</cite>

signals.title: Iran peace hopes drive oil retreat, but geopolitical risk persists
signals.desc: <cite index="12-7,13-14,25-18">Oil prices fell after reports Iran sent a new proposal through Pakistani mediators, though Trump maintains the naval blockade will stay until the Strait of Hormuz reopens, leaving energy-driven inflation risks elevated.</cite>

verdict: Markets are pricing peak AI-capex anxiety while ignoring a hawkish Fed—tech leadership remains intact but vulnerable to any stumble in monetization narratives.
